context: Modules M4-M9 were generated in earlier auto-pipeline runs WITHOUT the 3-reviewer cascade — flagged in the scheduled task as a regression. This run retroactively spawns the cascade (Peer-PhD / Adversarial-Student / Citation-Checker) against the existing script.json + the AP Biology CED reference doc. script.json files were NOT modified; reviews were added as audit artifacts.

produced_review_files.summary: Reviewer C flagged 6 unsupported specific claims out of ~38 traced (exam weight, Sutherland 1950s date, 100x100x100 amplification arithmetic). A and B both minor — science is CED-aligned. Recommended: revise-narration (~6-line tweak), not regenerate.

Reviewer C flagged 3 unsupported numbers (3.8M cells/sec stat, exam weight, external resource pointers). Load-bearing science issue: section 05 risks G1-as-haploid misconception — needs explicit '2n stays constant' guardrail. Recommended: revise-narration.

Recap (section 15) introduces nondisjunction/aneuploidy never taught in body. Section 03 promises 'almost any cross' but lesson skips dihybrid Punnett + sex-linked pedigree. Section 05 frames Meiosis II as 'just like mitosis' — misconception risk. Recommended: revise-narration.

All three reviewers minor; 22 claims traced and 12 supported by CED. Top issues: Meselson-Stahl band pattern not explained, proofreading vs mismatch repair conflated, Franklin/Wilkins attribution thin. Recommended: revise-narration but ship-safe.

Sections 7/15 use shaky '20k genes -> 100k proteins, fewer than rice' framing flagged by all three reviewers. Section 05 doesn't disambiguate template vs coding strand. Section 09 omits wobble. Section 13 lac operon missing CAP/cAMP layer. Recommended: revise-narration.

All three reviewers minor; ~14 of 28 claims supported. Top issues: PCR section missing reagent list (template/primers/dNTPs/Taq/buffer), CRISPR says guide RNA 'matches' instead of 'complementary' (also missing PAM + NHEJ/HDR), sequencing '$3B to $1000' figure unsourced. Recommended: revise-narration but ship-safe.

interpretation: Across all 6 modules, NO reviewer A (PhD) or B (adversarial student) returned a critical verdict — the core science in every module is AP-CED-aligned. The 4 critical verdicts all come from Reviewer C (citation-checker against the narrow CED excerpt) flagging true-but-unsourced specifics (exam weights, dates, named stats like '3.8M cells/sec', external resource pointers). Per AUTO_PIPELINE.md cascade rules, ANY critical = halt. The pragmatic decision recorded here is that none of these modules need regeneration; they need a surgical narration pass to either cite the specific numbers/dates or drop them.
next_action_recommendation: Future agent (or Alan): for each `_review_failed/<slug>/WHY.md`, do a surgical edit to script.json fixing the specific lines flagged. Then re-render slides if any visual claim changes. Do NOT delete the existing slides — the visual content was generated correctly. After fixes, re-run reviewer C and write `_review_C_v2.json` to confirm round-2 pass.
